faq_dropdown.a_proprietary_trading_firm_: A proprietary trading firm allocates its own capital to traders. Traders may be evaluated first, then earn a share of profits while following the firm’s risk rules.
faq_dropdown.getting_started_is_simple:
Getting started is simple:
Choose your preferred program/account size and trading setup.
Complete the Whalebase Trading Challenge by following the rules and reaching the profit objectives.
After you pass, we’ll review your performance and complete eligibility checks where required.
Once approved, you’ll receive access to your funded account and can begin trading under the Whalebase profit split model.
faq_dropdown.how_do_i_become_a_funded_tr: How do I become a funded trader?
faq_dropdown.a_one_step_challenge_or:
a one-step challenge, or
a two-step process (challenge + verification).
During these stages, you trade a simulated environment under Whalebase rules to demonstrate risk control and consistency.
faq_dropdown.if_eligible_you_submit_a_pa:
If eligible, you submit a payout request from your dashboard. Eligibility usually depends on:
Minimum profit threshold
Minimum trading days
Compliance with all rules
Passing any payout review checks
faq_dropdown.do_traders_owe_whalebase_mo: Do traders owe Whalebase money if they lose?
faq_dropdown.no_traders_are_generally_no: No. Traders are generally not liable for losses beyond the evaluation fee, as long as they follow the program rules.
